JACOBSON THE RIGHT TO INTERNALLY APPEAL THAT DECISION COUPLED WITH THE ISSUANCE OF THE FINAL DECISION DATED JULY 24, 2017 SIGNED BY DR. JACOBSON HAS BEEN REINSTATED AND IS SCHEDULED TO BE REMOVED EFFECTIVE JULY 31, 2017, THERE IS NOTHING LEFT OF THE WRIT APPEAL TO BE DETERMINED BY THE COURT OF APPEAL. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LESTER JACOBSON'S MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SETTING ASIDE OR DELAYING HIS REMOVAL FROM THE FACULTY OF THE INPATIENT MEDICINE TEACHING SERVICE IS DENIED. DR. JACOBSON HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HE HAS A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON HIS CONTENTION THAT THE PROCEDURES IN THE INTERNAL APPEAL WERE INSUFFICIENT TO COMPLY WITH THE COMMON LAW OF FAIR PROCEDURE. THE BALANCE OF HARMS IS EQUAL FOR BOTH DR. JACOBSON AND DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS. DENIAL OF THIS MOTION RESULTS IN DR. JACOBSON SUFFERING SIGNIFICANT HARM IN NOT BEING ABLE TO PARTICIPATE IN THE FACULTY OF THE INPATIENT MEDICINE TEACHING SERVICE. IF THE MOTION WERE GRANTED, CPMC WOULD SUFFER SIGNIFICANT HARM IN NOT BEING ABLE TO ORGANIZE ITS INPATIENT MEDICINE TEACHING SERVICE IN THE WAY THAT IT BELIEVES IS BEST FOR THE RESIDENTS, PATIENTS, STAFF AND ITSELF. BECAUSE THE HARMS TO EACH SIDE ARE EQUALLY BALANCED, THE DECIDING FACTOR IS THE LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. THE COURT CONTINUES THE AUGUST 7 TRIAL DATE TO DECEMBER 11, 2017 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 206. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DR. JACOBSON 'S MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO MAKING A MOTION SEEKING SOME OR ALL OF THE SAME FEES SOUGHT BY THIS MOTION ON A GROUND OTHER THAN CCP 1021.5 OR, IN THE EVENT OF AN APPEAL DECISION FAVORABLE TO DR. JACOBSON, BASED ON SECTION 1021.5. THE SOLE BASIS ASSERTED FOR AN AWARD OF FEES IS SECTION 1021.5. WHILE DR. JACOBSON IS A SUCCESSFUL PARTY PER THAT STATUTE, THE WRIT HE OBTAINED REQUIRING HIS REINSTATEMENT DID NOT CONFER A SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT ON THE GENERAL PUBLIC OR A LARGE CLASS OF PERSONS, WHICH IS A REQUIREMENT FOR A SECTION 1021.5 FEE AWARD. DR. JACOBSON HAS NOT SHOWN NOR IS THERE ANY REASON TO BELIEVE THAT THE WRIT WILL AFFECT VERY MANY, IF ANY, OTHER PERSONS. A DIFFERENT RESULT MIGHT OBTAIN IN EVENT THE WRIT IS AFFIRMED IN A PUBLISHED APPELLATE DECISION AND THE LANGUAGE OF THAT DECISION WAS APPLICABLE TO A LARGE CLASS OF PERSONS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. THE COURT CONTINUES THE AUGUST 7 TRIAL DATE TO DECEMBER 11, 2017 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 206. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. Plaintiff Lester Jacobson'S Motion For Order And Award Of Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jul-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, J. Maxwell Cooper and Michael A. Gawley of the law offices of Kessenick Gamma & Free LLP (415.568.2015) appear as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorneys, Glenda Zarbock and Ann Mary Olson of the law offices of Hanson Bridgett LLP (415.777.3200) appear as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

After reviewing the cases cited by counsel for petitioner Lester Jacobson at the hearing on July 21, 2017, the court re-affirms its tentative ruling that Dr. Jacobson 's motion for an award of attorney's fees is denied without prejudice to making a motion seeking some or all of the same fees sought by this motion on a ground other than CCP 1021.5 or, in the event of an appeal decision favorable to Dr. Jacobson, based on section 1021.5. The sole basis asserted for an award of fees is section 1021.5. While Dr. Jacobson is a successful party per that statute, the writ he obtained requiring his reinstatement did not confer a significant benefit on the general public or a large class of persons, which is a requirement for a section 1021.5 fee award. Dr. Jacobson has not shown nor is there any reason to believe that the writ will affect very many, if any, other persons. A different result might obtain in event the writ is affirmed in a published appellate decision and the language of that decision was applicable to a large class of persons. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

The Court continues the August 7 trial date to December 11, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 206.

Ex-Parte Application To File Supplemental Complaint Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction And In The Alternative Order Setting Briefing Schedule And Ordering Cpmc To Postpone Hearing
Off Calendar Jul-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, J. Maxwell Cooper and Michael A. Gawley of the law offices of Kessenick Gamma & Free LLP (415.568.2015) appear as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorneys, Glenda Zarbock and Ann Mary Olson of the law offices of Hanson Bridgett LLP (415.777.3200) appear as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Lester Jacobson's motion for a preliminary injunction setting aside or delaying his removal from the faculty of the inpatient medicine teaching service is DENIED. Dr. Jacobson has not shown that he has a likelihood of prevailing on his contention that the procedures in the internal appeal were insufficient to comply with the common law of fair procedure. The balance of harms is equal for both Dr. Jacobson and defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals. Denial of this motion results in Dr. Jacobson suffering significant harm in not being able to participate in the faculty of the inpatient medicine teaching service. If the motion were granted, CPMC would suffer significant harm in not being able to organize its inpatient medicine teaching service in the way that it believes is best for the residents, patients, staff and itself. Because the harms to each side are equally balanced, the deciding factor is the likelihood of success. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

The Court continues the August 7 trial date to December 11, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 206.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)

Defendnat Sutter West Bay Hospital'S Motion For Clarification Of Order Granting Motion To Enforce Writ
Off Calendar Jul-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, J. Maxwell Cooper and Michael A. Gawley of the law offices of Kessenick Gamma & Free LLP (415.568.2015) appear as counsel for plaintiff

Attorneys, Glenda Zarbock and Ann Mary Olson of the law offices of Hanson Bridgett LLP (415.777.3200) appear as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals' motion for clarification raises the issue of what portions, if any, of plaintiff Lester Jacobson's complaint are stayed as a result of CPMC's appeal of the writ requiring reinstatement of Dr. Jacobson and what portions, if any, may proceed to trial notwithstanding CPMC's appeal. Dr. Edson's letter of June 19, 2017 removing Dr. Jacobson from the faculty of the inpatient medicine teaching service effective July 31, 2017 and affording Dr. Jacobson the right to internally appeal that decision coupled with the issuance of the final decision dated July 24, 2017 signed by Dr. Lee denying the internal appeal moots CPMC's appeal of the writ order. Now that Dr. Jacobson has been reinstated and is scheduled to be removed effective July 31, 2017, there is nothing left of the writ appeal to be determined by the Court of Appeal. Because CPMC's appeal is now mooted, that appeal no longer stays any proceedings in this case in this court. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

The Court continues the August 7 trial date to December 11, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 206.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Writ And If Necessary For Relief From Stay
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. J. MAXWELL COOPER OF THE KESSENICK GAMMA & FREE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-568-2015 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GLENDA ZARBOCK & ATTY. ANN MARY OLSON OF HANSON BRIDGETT, TEL. NO. 1-415-777-3200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CPMC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LESTER JACOBSON'S Motion To Enforce Writ And If Necessary For Relief From Stay is GRANTED. Per CCP 1110b, this court directs that the appeal of the order granting the writ does not operate as a stay of the execution of that order because Dr. Jacobson has shown that he will suffer irreparable damage to his profession as a physician if the execution of the order is stayed. This motion is not premature because the order granting writ required respondent Sutter West Bay Hospitals to immediately reinstate Dr. Jacobson in the inpatient teaching service. The order granting the writ was appealable per CCP 904.1(a)(6) as a mandatory injunction and consequently the appeal automatically stayed the execution of the order per CCP 916. Dr. Jacobson has shown good cause for relief from the stay of execution per CCP 1110b. Dr. Jacobson has shaped his practice around his participation in the inpatient teaching service, the inpatient teaching service conferred a substantial benefit on Dr. Jacobson and his removal from the service has impacted his ability to practice his profession and business in the way he has done for over 40 years. Moreover, Dr. Jacobson has shown reputational harm based on his absence from the teaching service after more than 40 years participating in that service and his having to tell patients he cannot provide continuous care for them once they are admitted to CPMC's hospital facilities. These harms are more than pecuniary in nature and warrant the granting of relief per CCP 1110b. CPMC is ordered to immediately comply with the peremptory writ. Dr. Jacobson's request for a fine of $1,000 against CPMC is denied. CPMC's filing of an appeal the day after the court issued its order, which automatically stayed the peremptory writ, constitutes sufficient grounds for CPMC's lack of compliance with the writ up to this point. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Withheld By Defendant Cpmc Under Claim Of Evidence Code 1157 Privilege
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, James Maxwell Cooper of Kessenick Gamma & Free, LLP (415-568-2015) appears for plaintiff/moving party, Lester Jacobson MD

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF LESTER JACOBSON'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Withheld By Defendant CPMC Under Claim Of Evidence Code 1157 Privilege is GRANTED. The Court adopts the substance of the judge pro tem's recommendations and thus GRANTS the motion, limited to documents relevant to plaintiff-petitioner and/or his claims. The discovery limitation of Evidence Code 1157 "does not apply'to any person requesting hospital staff privileges." (Id.) The Legislature defines "staff privileges" in the health-care context as "any arrangement under which a licentiate is allowed to practice in or provide care for patients in a health facility." (Bus. & Prof. Code 805.) The definition is followed by an extensive list of examples demonstrating - if any doubt there was - it is meant to be broad. (Id.) Plaintiff-petitioner's privileges to instruct and direct CPMC residents fit the Legislature's definition. Additionally, 90 of the 106 at-issue documents (those from the "GMEC") do not come within the ambit of Evidence Code 1157 at all. The parties are to meet and confer and then propose a protective order for the compelled documents. No sanctions are awarded; CPMC's positions - though I disagree with them - do not lack substantial justification. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745, email: lmartinez@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. SCOTT C. KESSENICK, ESQ. of Kessenick, Gamma & Free, LLP, Tel No. 415-568-2004 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Attys. GLENDA ZARBOCK, ESQ. and RACHEL P. ZURAW, ESQ. of Hanson Bridgett LLP, Tel No. 415-777-3200 appearing for the Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Defendant CPMC's demurrer to the amended complaint is overruled in its entirety and its request for judicial notice is denied. While the statements in ACGME's program requirements for internal medicine residents may be not reasonably subject to dispute, their meaning and application to this case are subject to dispute. All three claims are adequately pled. Dr. Jacobson states a cognizable claim for retaliation per Health and Safety Code 1278.5 by alleging that after he reported his concerns about how CPMC discharged some patients, CPMC took various actions against him. Dr. Jacobson also states a cognizable claim for violation of fair procedure by alleging that some of his medical staff privileges were eliminated causing him a financial loss without providing him adequate notice or opportunity to respond. Because Dr. Jacobson's Business and Professions Code 17200 claim is based on the first two claims and Dr. Jacobson has standing to assert such a claim due to his alleged financial loss, he has also stated a cognizable 17200 claim. See also the ruling on CPMC's demurrer to the petition for writ of mandate. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
